Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Flip Distance of Convex Triangulations and Tree Rotation Is NP-Complete 22 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Moss is a pixel canvas where every brush is a tiny program 211 upvotes, 25 comments
  3. NanoGPT Slowrun: Language Modeling with Limited Data, Infinite Compute 137 upvotes, 26 comments
  4. Qwen3.5 Fine-Tuning Guide 306 upvotes, 70 comments
  5. Building a new Flash 429 upvotes, 119 comments
  6. Libre Solar – Open Hardware for Renewable Energy 230 upvotes, 67 comments
  7. MyFirst Kids Watch Hacked. Access to Camera and Microphone 123 upvotes, 33 comments
  8. “It turns out” (2010) 261 upvotes, 84 comments
  9. The View from RSS 94 upvotes, 26 comments
  10. An interactive map of Flock Cams 545 upvotes, 199 comments
  11. Jensen Huang says Nvidia is pulling back from OpenAI and Anthropic 65 upvotes, 18 comments
  12. Something is afoot in the land of Qwen 590 upvotes, 262 comments
  13. Google Workspace CLI 288 upvotes, 122 comments
  14. Raspberry Pi Pico as AM Radio Transmitter 84 upvotes, 31 comments
  15. Daemon (2006) 34 upvotes, 10 comments
  16. Dario Amodei calls OpenAI’s messaging around military deal ‘straight up lies’ 383 upvotes, 193 comments
  17. A bit of fluid mechanics from scratch not from scratch 39 upvotes, 13 comments
  18. Humans 40k yrs ago developed a system of conventional signs 87 upvotes, 39 comments
  19. NRC issues first commercial reactor construction approval in 10 years [pdf] 75 upvotes, 33 comments
  20. Chaos and Dystopian news for the dead internet survivors 61 upvotes, 27 comments
  21. Glaze by Raycast 207 upvotes, 125 comments
  22. Picking Up a Zillion Pieces of Litter 62 upvotes, 33 comments
  23. Malm Whale 16 upvotes, 5 comments
  24. Was Windows 1.0's lack of overlapping windows a legal or a technical matter? 69 upvotes, 45 comments
  25. Completing the formal proof of higher-dimensional sphere packing 6 upvotes, 1 comments
  26. BMW Group to deploy humanoid robots in production in Germany for the first time 101 upvotes, 81 comments
  27. US tech firms pledge at White House to bear costs of energy for datacenters 53 upvotes, 39 comments
  28. What Python's asyncio primitives get wrong about shared state 19 upvotes, 10 comments
  29. MacBook Neo 1666 upvotes, 1963 comments
  30. Roboflow (YC S20) Is Hiring a Security Engineer for AI Infra 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 1:00pm, 5 March 2026. Source here.
